reopen_rule: Satisfied lower gates may only be reopened by current regression receipt.
workstream_objective: No counted R6 work is executable until a future same-head router-versus-best-adapter proof actually earns router superiority. Until then, only governed noncanonical preparation may continue across crucibles, adapters, tournaments, merge, and router-shadow improvement without cutover, lobe promotion, externality, comparative, or commercial widening.

gate_domain_product_split.gate: Gate D sixth ordered ratification step remains blocked
gate_domain_product_split.domain_surface: Gate D may discuss learned-router authorization only after router superiority is actually earned on a future same-head R5 proof chain.

allowed_noncanonical_parallelism: crucible strengthening that does not widen externality or canonical authority
adapter, merge, and tournament improvement that remains inside Gate D lab scope
router shadow refinement that remains replayable, explainable, and cutover-blocked
stop_conditions: Any attempt to execute B04_R6_LEARNED_ROUTER_AUTHORIZATION while router superiority remains unearned.
Any claim that the R5 static hold authorizes learned-router cutover or lobe promotion.
Any externality widening above E1, external comparative claim, or commercial activation is folded into this blocked state.
Any broader lab improvement work is narrated as counted canonical advancement.

pass: R6 remains blocked until same-head router superiority is actually earned.
The static canonical router hold remains stated honestly with no learned-router cutover or lobe promotion.
Only governed noncanonical preparation remains open while externality, comparative, and commercial widening stay blocked.
fail: R6 is treated as executable without earned router superiority proof.
The post-R5 hold is narrated as learned-router authority or lobe readiness.
Broader lab work is counted as canonical Gate D advancement.
